name: agent-toolkit
description: Curated agents, skills, and hooks for web development

agents.name: accessibility
agents.description: Accessibility specialist focused on WCAG 2.1 compliance, assistive technology compatibility, and inclusive design

agents.name: architect
agents.description: Strategic architecture advisor for system design decisions, scalability, and technical trade-offs. Read-only analysis with ADR support.

agents.name: brainstorming
agents.description: Design-first exploration before implementation. Explores user intent, requirements, and design through collaborative dialogue before any code is written.

agents.name: browser-automator
agents.description: AI-driven browser automation agent using browser-use. Navigates, scrapes, fills forms, tests web apps, and extracts data autonomously with domain restriction and sensitive data handling.

agents.name: build-error-resolver
agents.description: Build and TypeScript error resolution specialist. Fixes build/type errors with minimal diffs. No architectural changes. Gets the build green quickly.

agents.name: code-reviewer
agents.description: Expert code review with severity-rated feedback, logic defect detection, SOLID checks, and security focus. Use after writing or modifying code.

agents.name: database-reviewer
agents.description: Database/SQL review specialist for query optimization, schema design, security, and performance. Covers PostgreSQL patterns, migrations, and RLS.

agents.name: doc-updater
agents.description: Documentation and codemap specialist. Updates codemaps, READMEs, and guides to match the current state of the codebase.

agents.name: e2e-runner
agents.description: End-to-end testing specialist using Playwright. Generates, maintains, and runs E2E tests. Manages test journeys, quarantines flaky tests, and ensures critical user flows work.

agents.name: explorer
agents.description: Codebase exploration specialist that traces execution paths, maps architecture layers, and documents dependencies to inform new development.

agents.name: fastapi-reviewer
agents.description: Reviews FastAPI applications for async correctness, dependency injection, Pydantic schemas, security, OpenAPI quality, and production readiness.

agents.name: loop-operator
agents.description: Operates autonomous agent loops, monitors progress, and intervenes safely when loops stall. Manages quality gates, eval baselines, and rollback paths.

agents.name: performance-optimizer
agents.description: Performance analysis and optimization specialist. Identifies bottlenecks, optimizes slow code, reduces bundle sizes, and improves runtime performance.

agents.name: planner
agents.description: Expert planning specialist for complex features and refactoring. Creates detailed, actionable implementation plans with exact file paths and step-by-step instructions.

agents.name: python-reviewer
agents.description: Expert Python code reviewer specializing in PEP 8, Pythonic idioms, type hints, security, and performance. Use for all Python code changes.

agents.name: react-reviewer
agents.description: Expert React/Next.js code reviewer specializing in hook correctness, render performance, server/client boundaries, accessibility, and React-specific security.

agents.name: refactor-cleaner
agents.description: Dead code cleanup and consolidation specialist. Removes unused code, duplicates, and refactors safely using analysis tools (knip, depcheck, ts-prune).

agents.name: security-reviewer
agents.description: Security vulnerability detection and remediation. Flags secrets, SSRF, injection, unsafe crypto, and OWASP Top 10. Use after writing code handling user input, auth, API endpoints, or sensitive data.

agents.name: senior-fullstack
agents.description: Senior full-stack web developer with expertise in W3C, WCAG, OWASP, and modern web frameworks

agents.name: senior-ux
agents.description: Senior UX expert specializing in user experience research, interaction design, and accessibility

agents.name: subagent-driven-dev
agents.description: Execute implementation plans by dispatching focused subagents per task with two-stage review (spec compliance then code quality).

agents.name: systematic-debugging
agents.description: Structured root-cause analysis for bugs, test failures, and unexpected behavior. ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes.

agents.name: tdd-guide
agents.description: Test-Driven Development specialist enforcing write-tests-first methodology. Use proactively when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring. Ensures 80%+ test coverage.

agents.name: typescript-reviewer
agents.description: Expert TypeScript/JavaScript code reviewer specializing in type safety, async correctness, and idiomatic patterns. Use for all TS/JS code changes.

agents.name: verification-before-completion
agents.description: Final quality gate before claiming work is complete. Requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims. Evidence before assertions, always.

agents.name: writing-plans
agents.description: Creates comprehensive implementation plans with exact file paths, code, and commands. Use after design approval, before touching code.

skills.name: agent-eval
skills.description: Use when comparing AI agents or coding tools against each other, measuring pass rates, cost, and consistency across standardized tasks.

skills.name: ai-coding-agents
skills.description: Use when choosing, configuring, or integrating AI coding agents (Aider, Cline, Continue, OpenHands, Roo Code, Plandex, Goose, Tabby, Void, SWE-agent, or alternatives like free-claude-code). Covers terminal agents, IDE extensions, autonomous agents, and self-hosted solutions.

skills.name: api-design
skills.description: Use when designing new REST APIs, creating endpoints, or defining response formats and pagination strategies.

skills.name: architecture-decision-records
skills.description: Use when making significant architectural choices, selecting technologies, or designing system boundaries that affect future development.

skills.name: backend-patterns
skills.description: Use when building Node.js APIs, managing database queries, implementing caching, or handling errors in backend services.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Use before any creative work — creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements, and design before implementation.

skills.name: browser-use
skills.description: Use when automating web browsers with AI agents — scraping, form filling, testing, data extraction, multi-step web workflows, or any task requiring browser interaction via browser-use (Python).

skills.name: codebase-onboarding
skills.description: Use when starting work on an unfamiliar codebase, joining a new project, or creating onboarding documentation.

skills.name: context-budget
skills.description: Use when context window is getting full, when agents or skills are consuming too many tokens, or when auditing the efficiency of your Claude Code configuration.

skills.name: continuous-learning-v2
skills.description: Use when you notice patterns in your work that should be captured as project-level or global instincts. Observes corrections, patterns, and preferences to build a self-improving knowledge base.

skills.name: deep-paper-note
skills.description: Use when deep-reading an academic paper and generating structured Obsidian-style research notes. Handles paper identity resolution, metadata extraction, figure/table placement, mechanism breakdown, and evidence-based analysis.

skills.name: executing-plans
skills.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute with review checkpoints.

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.description: Use when implementation is complete and all tests pass. Guides merge, PR, or cleanup decisions.

skills.name: frontend-a11y
skills.description: Use when building interactive UI components, forms, or navigation to ensure keyboard accessibility, screen reader support, and ARIA compliance.

skills.name: frontend-design-direction
skills.description: Use when starting a new UI project, page, or component that needs visual direction before implementation. Establishes design purpose, audience, tone, and constraints.

skills.name: frontend-patterns
skills.description: Use when building React/Next.js components, managing state, handling forms, or optimizing frontend performance.

skills.name: ralphex
skills.description: Use when autonomously executing implementation plans with Claude Code or Codex. Handles multi-phase code review, git branching, worktree isolation, and plan tracking. Extended Ralph loop for production-grade autonomous coding.

skills.name: react-patterns
skills.description: Best practices and patterns for React 19 and Next.js 15+ development

skills.name: react-performance
skills.description: Use when optimizing React rendering, reducing bundle size, improving data fetching, or diagnosing slow React applications.

skills.name: react-testing
skills.description: Testing patterns for React components and Next.js applications

skills.name: receiving-code-review
skills.description: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions. Requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement.

skills.name: reflect
skills.description: Use when users provide corrections, express preferences, or when patterns emerge from successful approaches. Analyzes conversations to extract learnings and propose skill improvements.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging, to verify work meets requirements.

skills.name: security-review
skills.description: Use before deploying web applications, after adding authentication/authorization, when handling user input, or when setting up API endpoints.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks. Dispatches fresh subagent per task with two-stage review.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.description: Four-phase root cause analysis for complex bugs — trace, isolate, verify, fix

skills.name: tdd-workflow
skills.description: Use when writing new features, fixing bugs, or refactoring in a web project. Enforces TDD with 80%+ coverage and git checkpoints.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code. Enforces RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle.

skills.name: using-git-worktrees
skills.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from the current workspace, or before executing implementation plans.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs. Requires running verification commands before making success claims.

skills.name: vvveb-js
skills.description: Use when building drag-and-drop page builders, WYSIWYG editors, or visual HTML composition tools. VvvebJs is a vanilla JS page builder library with zero dependencies, Bootstrap 5 components, undo/redo, live code editing, and ZIP export.

skills.name: web-security-essentials
skills.description: Use when building web applications, setting up authentication, handling user input, configuring headers, or deploying to production. Covers OWASP Top 10 essentials for web developers.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.description: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment.

commands.name: code-review
commands.description: Run a comprehensive code review on recent changes

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Create an implementation plan for a task

commands.name: reflect
commands.description: Extract learnings from the current session

commands.name: security-scan
commands.description: Quick security scan for common vulnerabilities
